proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +42.5pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.61 remains under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance); watch for tier drift if R-multiple recomputes lower on future archive
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +42.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +42.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below the Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 5), so the cohort is dormant and the flagged 100% Choppy concentration is not compounded by a Bear-gated promotion. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-07-12) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +29.9pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.98 sits just below Tier S threshold and is under D-8 review for Day-6 forced-exit dominance; if forced-exit rule changes, R-multiple may shift materially and warrant re-tiering.
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +29.9pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +29.9pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 5), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is 0 and the flagged 100% Choppy concentration is on a different regime, so a Bear-gated BULL_PROXY promotion does not compound concentration risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 07-12) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime + by_signal_type×regime×sector = Choppy regime (100%); five cells each at 20% share (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime + by_signal_type×regime×sector = Choppy regime (100%); five cells each at 20% share; recommended action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until either regime rotates or at least 2 positions exit (Day 6 natural exit); prefer any qualifying non-Choppy or non-UP_TRI signal for the next addition to reduce the cell concentration.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: If regime shifts abruptly from Choppy to Bear or a volatility expansion event, all 5 positions correlate to the downside simultaneously with no offsetting exposure — cohort edge is unvalidated (Candidate tier only), so there is no historical basis to expect the concentration to be compensated by superior WR.

evidence: regime_concentration.triggered=true, observed=1.0 vs threshold 0.80 (all 5 open in Choppy)
All 5 concentration cells at 0.20 share — meets the 0.20 cell threshold mechanically
UP_TRI×Choppy cohort at Candidate tier (n=294) — unvalidated edge
BULL_PROXY×Choppy cohort at Candidate tier (n=31) — unvalidated edge
UP_TRI signal share at 4/5 = 80%, approaching 0.85 monoculture threshold
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.concentration_value: Choppy regime (100%); five cells each at 20% share
decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until either regime rotates or at least 2 positions exit (Day 6 natural exit); prefer any qualifying non-Choppy or non-UP_TRI signal for the next addition to reduce the cell concentration.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: If regime shifts abruptly from Choppy to Bear or a volatility expansion event, all 5 positions correlate to the downside simultaneously with no offsetting exposure — cohort edge is unvalidated (Candidate tier only), so there is no historical basis to expect the concentration to be compensated by superior WR.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Regime concentration is triggered at 1.0 (100% Choppy, well above 0.80 threshold) with all five open positions in Choppy regime, and every concentration cell sits at 20% share — mechanically breaching the 0.20 cell threshold. Matched Choppy×UP_TRI and Choppy×BULL_PROXY cohorts are only Candidate tier (n=294, n=31) — unvalidated edge. UP_TRI also dominates signal mix at 4/5 = 80% (below the 0.85 threshold but close).
